title: Pressure on GAE 63K films
description: Apply 2-5 GPa pressure to the GAE-grown 63K (La,Pr)₃Ni₂O₇ films. The cited film-pressure series reports 62→68.5K with just 2 GPa on lower-quality films. If GAE films with fewer oxygen vacancies follow that empirical trend, a hypothesis-generating scenario range is 75-85K.
risk: Low — piston-cylinder cells are standard equipment

title: GAE on SmAlO₃
description: GAE-grown (La,Pr)₃Ni₂O₇ on SmAlO₃ (a=3.734Å, strain ≈ -2.6%). If the c/a trend holds beyond the measured range, the extra 0.6% compression could increase onset Tc by ~10-15K. Key risk: film coherence at higher mismatch; no data exists for this substrate.
risk: Medium — SmAlO₃ substrates available but film coherence at -2.6% strain is uncharted

title: Sr-doping × GAE on SLAO
description: Sr-doping at optimal level (x≈0.15) + GAE growth on SLAO. The dome study (PRL 2026) used MBE, not GAE. Combining the best doping level with the best growth method has not been done.
risk: Medium — requires precise doping control during GAE growth

title: Electric field gating
description: Electric field gating of single-bilayer films. Theory predicts 0.1-0.2V perpendicular voltage should enhance intralayer d-wave pairing enough to reach 77K+. Zero attempts exist.
risk: High — theoretical prediction only, ionic liquid gating on nickelates untested

title: Superstructures on high-compression substrates
description: Engineer superstructures on a substrate with a < 3.75Å — test whether stronger strain preserves the γII Fermi pocket while pushing Tc higher. The Nature paper (Apr 9, 2026) reports γII as an SC-associated signature; verify the original figures before using it as a screening criterion.
risk: High — unknown if γII pocket survives at extreme strain
